people.family.me: This one is you! It's how you talk about yourself.
When you press it, you're telling someone about you.
Try it like this: 'Me!' or 'It's me.'
people.family.mom: Your mom is the grown-up who loves you and takes care of you.
She hugs you, feeds you, and is there when you need her.
Try it like this: 'I want Mom.'
people.family.dad: Your dad is the grown-up who loves you and takes care of you.
He plays with you, holds you, and is there when you need him.
Try it like this: 'I want Dad.'
people.family.brother: Your brother is a boy in your family, with your same parents.
He's family — you live together, share, and play.
Try it like this: 'That's my brother.'
people.family.sister: Your sister is a girl in your family, with your same parents.
She's family — you live together, share, and play.
Try it like this: 'That's my sister.'
people.family.baby: A baby is the littlest person in the family, brand new.
Babies are small and soft — they sleep a lot and need gentle care.
Try it like this: 'Look at the baby.'
people.family.grandma: Your grandma is your mom's mom or your dad's mom.
She loves you, spoils you a little, and gives the best hugs.
Try it like this: 'I want to see Grandma.'
people.family.grandpa: Your grandpa is your mom's dad or your dad's dad.
He loves you, tells stories, and likes spending time with you.
Try it like this: 'I want to see Grandpa.'
people.community.friend: A friend is someone you like to be with and play with.
You share, take turns, and have fun together.
Try it like this: 'You're my friend.'
people.community.workers.mechanic: A mechanic is a person who fixes cars and trucks.
They check the engine, change tires, and make cars run again.
Try it like this: 'The mechanic fixed our car.'
people.community.workers.chef: A chef is a person who cooks food at a restaurant.
They mix, fry, and bake to make tasty meals.
Try it like this: 'The chef made my dinner.'
people.community.workers.farmer: A farmer is a person who grows food and raises animals.
They plant crops and care for cows, pigs, and chickens.
Try it like this: 'The farmer grows our food.'
people.community.workers.plumber: A plumber is a person who fixes pipes and sinks.
They stop leaks and make the water flow the right way.
Try it like this: 'The plumber fixed the sink.'
people.team.therapist: A therapist is a special helper who works with you to learn and grow.
They play games with you to help you talk, move, or feel calm.
Try it like this: 'I see my therapist today.'
needs.feelings.tired: Tired is the heavy, sleepy feeling when your body wants to rest.
Your eyes droop and you might yawn — you need a break or a nap.
Try it like this: 'I'm tired' or 'I want to rest.'
needs.feelings.more.embarrassed: Embarrassed is the warm, shy feeling that makes you wish the moment was private.
Your cheeks get hot and you might want to hide a little.
Try it like this: 'I feel embarrassed.'
needs.feelings.more.confused: Confused is the mixed-up feeling that means you need it explained another way.
Your brain is asking for more clues so it can put the pieces together.
Try it like this: 'I'm confused' or 'Can you show me again?'
needs.feelings.more.bored: Bored is the antsy feeling that means your body is ready for something fun.
You're looking for an adventure, a toy, or a place to go.
Try it like this: 'I'm bored' or 'I want something to do.'
needs.feelings.more.nervous: Nervous is the jittery feeling when you're worried about something.
Your tummy flutters and your hands might feel wiggly.
Try it like this: 'I feel nervous.'
needs.feelings.more.lonely: Lonely is the sad feeling when you wish someone were with you.
You want company — a friend, a hug, or someone to play.
Try it like this: 'I feel lonely' or 'I want someone.'
needs.feelings.more.angry: Angry is the hot, big feeling when something is unfair or wrong.
Your body gets tight and you might want to stomp or yell.
Try it like this: 'I'm angry' or 'I'm so mad.'
needs.feelings.extra.jealous: Jealous is the achy feeling when someone has what you wish you had.
You want a turn too, and it stings to wait or share.
Try it like this: 'I feel jealous.'
needs.feelings.extra.disappointed: Disappointed is the let-down feeling when something hoped-for didn't happen.
You wanted it, and it turned out a different way.
Try it like this: 'I feel disappointed.'
needs.feelings.extra.grateful: Grateful is the warm feeling of being thankful for something good.
You're glad for what you have or what someone did for you.
Try it like this: 'I feel grateful' or 'Thank you.'
needs.feelings.extra.brave: Brave is the strong feeling that helps you do something even when it's scary.
Your heart is a little nervous, but you try anyway.
Try it like this: 'I'm being brave.'
needs.feelings.extra.curious: Curious is the excited feeling of wanting to know more.
You want to look, touch, and find out how things work.
Try it like this: 'I'm curious' or 'What's that?'
needs.feelings.extra.hopeful: Hopeful is the bright feeling that something good might happen.
You're looking forward, wishing for the best.
Try it like this: 'I feel hopeful.'
nouns.health.sore: Sore is the achy, hurting feeling in a part of your body.
It can come after you fall, run a lot, or feel sick.
Try it like this: 'It's sore' or 'My leg hurts.'
nouns.health.sneeze: A sneeze is a big, fast 'achoo!' that bursts out of your nose.
It happens all of a sudden when your nose feels tickly.
Try it like this: 'I have to sneeze' or 'Achoo!'
nouns.health.cough: A cough is a rough sound that pops out to clear your throat.
It can happen when you're sick or something tickles your throat.
Try it like this: 'I have a cough.'
nouns.health.medicine: Medicine is something a grown-up gives you to help you feel better.
You take a little when you're sick, and it helps your body heal.
Try it like this: 'I need my medicine.'
personal.therapy.therapy_room: The therapy room is a special room where you work with your helper.
It has toys and tools that make practicing fun.
Try it like this: 'We go to the therapy room.'
personal.therapy.session_start: Session start is the beginning of your time with your helper.
You say hello and get ready to play and practice.
Try it like this: 'We're starting now.'
personal.therapy.session_end: Session end is when your time with your helper is finished.
You clean up, say goodbye, and head out.
Try it like this: 'All done with therapy.'
personal.therapy.reinforcer: A reinforcer is a fun reward you get for trying hard.
After good work, you earn a turn with something you love.
Try it like this: 'I want my reward.'
personal.therapy.sensory_bin: A sensory bin is a tub full of stuff to dig your hands into.
It might have beans, rice, or sand to scoop and squish.
Try it like this: 'I want the sensory bin.'
personal.therapy.visual_schedule: A visual schedule is a row of pictures that shows what comes next.
You look at it to know the plan for your time.
Try it like this: 'What's next on my schedule?'
personal.therapy.target_word: A target word is the special word you're practicing right now.
You say it again and again to make it stronger.
Try it like this: 'I said my word!'
personal.therapy.co_treat: A co-treat partner is another helper who joins your session.
Two helpers work with you together at the same time.
Try it like this: 'We have two helpers today.'
personal.therapy.quiet_space: A quiet space is a calm, soft spot to settle your body.
You can go there to breathe and feel calm when things are too much.
Try it like this: 'I need the quiet space.'
personal.therapy.therapy_toy: This is the toy you love best in the therapy room.
You get to play with it as a treat for your hard work.
Try it like this: 'I want my favorite toy.'
personal.family.bedtime_routine: Bedtime is when the day ends and you go to sleep.
You put on pajamas, brush your teeth, and snuggle in.
Try it like this: 'It's bedtime' or 'One more book, please.'
personal.family.grandma_house: Grandma's house is the place where your grandma lives.
You visit to see her, get hugs, and feel cozy.
Try it like this: 'We're going to Grandma's house.'
personal.family.grandpa_house: Grandpa's house is the place where your grandpa lives.
You visit to see him, play, and feel cozy.
Try it like this: 'We're going to Grandpa's house.'
personal.family.comfort_object: Your lovey is the special soft thing you snuggle for comfort.
It might be a blanket or a stuffed animal you love best.
Try it like this: 'I want my lovey.'
personal.family.family_pet: Your pet is the animal that lives with your family.
You feed it, pet it, and love it like part of the family.
Try it like this: 'I want to see our pet.'
personal.family.family_car: Your car is the vehicle your family rides in to go places.
You buckle up in your seat and ride along.
Try it like this: 'Let's get in the car.'
personal.family.our_park: This is the park your family likes to go to.
You know the swings and slides there because you visit a lot.
Try it like this: 'I want to go to our park.'
personal.family.special_snack: Your special snack is a treat you really love to eat.
It's the one you ask for and look forward to most.
Try it like this: 'I want my special snack.'
personal.family.bath_time: Bath time is when you get clean in the warm water.
You splash, play with bubbles, and wash up.
Try it like this: 'It's bath time!'
personal.family.favorite_book: Your favorite book is the story you love to read again and again.
You know the pictures and want it read at bedtime.
Try it like this: 'Read my favorite book.'
personal.media.favorite_show: Your show is the TV program you love to watch.
You know the characters and ask to see it.
Try it like this: 'I want to watch my show.'
personal.media.favorite_movie: Your movie is the film you love to watch over and over.
You know all the parts and wait for your favorite scene.
Try it like this: 'I want my movie.'
personal.media.favorite_song: Your song is the music you love to hear and move to.
You light up and want to dance when it comes on.
Try it like this: 'Play my song!'
personal.media.favorite_character: Your character is the one from a show or book you love most.
You look for them in toys, books, and shows.
Try it like this: 'That's my character!'
personal.media.favorite_app: Your app is the game or program you love on a screen.
You tap and play it on a tablet or phone.
Try it like this: 'I want my app.'
